first.name: Yerlan T.
first.subtitle: Brand founder
first.title: The team boosted our sales
first.body: We came in without a clear strategy and in two months got a steady flow of leads. The team onboarded quickly, explained every step, and took full ownership of marketing.

second.name: Maria K.
second.subtitle: Project lead
second.title: Clear, transparent, and practical
second.body: I liked the structured approach from analytics to ad creatives. We see the metrics, understand where the budget goes, and get results without extra routine in-house.

smm.description: We build engaged communities around your brand, grow organic traffic, and turn followers into buyers—systematically, without fake engagement.

design.description: Shapes the brand’s visual voice—from strategy down to the pixel. Turns ideas into content people remember and share.

silpo.title: Silpo
silpo.description: End-to-end promotion for a major supermarket chain on Instagram and Google

1.title: Growth by metrics
1.description: We tracked core KPIs weekly: engagement, reach, cost per lead, and ad contribution to revenue. We reallocated budget, shut down weak ad sets, and scaled what consistently hit CPL and ROAS targets.

2.title: Steady reach
2.description: We built a content calendar—series, stories, reels, and feed posts mapped to funnel stages. We kept a steady publishing cadence and DM handling so organic reach grew without sharp drops and kept trust between paid pushes.

3.title: Budget optimization
3.description: We launched hypotheses with small budgets and scaled only when metrics stayed predictable. Retargeting and lookalikes were added in stages after events were set up and data quality in analytics checked out.

4.title: Transparent reporting
4.description: Every week: a short report with numbers, takeaways, and next sprint priorities. Commitments were recorded as tasks so clients always knew what was done, in progress, and why priorities shifted.

1.title: Goal
1.body: The goal wasn’t just polished social media—it was a system: attract attention, retain the audience, and move people to a goal action from follower to lead or purchase. Organic and paid had to reinforce each other, not compete, and avoid diluting the brand with mixed messages.

2.title: Approach
2.body: We started with a channel and analytics audit: events on site and in ad accounts, the full funnel, existing creatives and landing pages. Then we built a unified content engine and campaigns with clear hypotheses. Each stage tested offers, formats, and visuals to see what resonates with your audience—not generic benchmarks.

3.title: Outcome
3.body: Over time both reach and traffic quality improved: leads became steadier, not sporadic spikes. We gradually increased spend on proven ad sets and cut what didn’t deliver sustainable impact—scaling without chaotic budget growth.

4.title: Reporting
4.body: Clients got more than screenshots: a clear picture of what changed, what metrics moved, what we plan next, and risks we see in the market and creatives. Short regular syncs helped reprioritize quickly without a backlog of silent tasks.

sales.description: How we helped a regional restaurant scale nationally—from zero to 40,000 followers and 2.3M monthly reach.

1.question: How quickly do you launch campaigns after we start?
1.answer: Usually a basic launch takes 3-7 business days: we gather inputs, check analytics, form hypotheses, and launch the first ad sets. Complex projects with many channels can take up to 10-14 days.

2.question: Which advertising channels do you use?
2.answer: We pick channels for the goal: Meta Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, SEO, email marketing, and content. Each project gets a mix that delivers the best cost per lead and sales outcome.

3.question: How do you measure ad performance?
3.answer: We lock KPIs before launch: CPL, CPA, ROAS, CAC, and conversion to lead and sale. Then we report weekly and monthly so you see trends and decide based on data, not guesses.

4.question: Do you only work with large companies?
4.answer: No. We work with businesses of all sizes—from small local companies to international projects. Strategy and budget adapt to your growth stage.

5.question: Who actually runs my project?
5.answer: A dedicated marketer owns the project with specialists for targeting, paid search, design, analytics, and content. You always know who owns each task and deadline.

6.question: Can we start with a trial period?
6.answer: Yes. We can start with a testing phase to validate hypotheses and find winning setups. After the test we build a scalable plan and a clear forecast for further growth.

footer.quoteBold: We're ready to work for you,
footer.quoteRest: so you get the best outcome at a fair price
footer.about: End-to-end business promotion. We work with companies worldwide—from startups to large enterprises.
